ux_issues.description: Selection mode response was 5692 characters — very long for a user sitting at an airport gate about to board. The AI proactively created 6 itinerary items, did 2 web searches, handed off to a food expert, AND called recommend_booking all in a single turn. While thorough, this is too much for a last-minute traveler who just needs 'help me fast'. The companion mode responses (692-875 chars) were much better calibrated.
ux_issues.suggestion: In selection mode, when urgency signals are present (e.g., 'right now', 'boards in 2 hours'), the AI should create the trip and give a brief acknowledgment, deferring detailed planning to follow-up messages rather than front-loading everything.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: The initial selection-mode response attempted to build the entire trip plan in one message instead of just creating the trip and giving a quick acknowledgment. It called 7 tools (create_trip, 2x web_search, 2x create_itinerary_items, suggest_expert, recommend_booking) in a single turn. For a user who said 'I'm at the airport right now', this is overkill — create the trip, give 2-3 bullet points, and let the user drive.

The AI did not mention that the user is a photographer or suggest photography-optimized spots (morning light at Alfama, golden hour viewpoints). The persona mentions Jake is a freelance photographer, but this was only in the persona background — the AI wouldn't know this from the chat message alone. Not a bug, just a note.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI excelled in companion mode — responses were concise (692-875 chars), practical, and full of specific Lisbon knowledge (Cervejaria Ramiro, Pasteis de Belem, metro Red Line to airport, Bolt/Uber pricing). The trip was created instantly without unnecessary clarifying questions, which is exactly right for a last-minute traveler. The only weakness was the selection mode response being too verbose and tool-heavy for the urgency of the situation. Companion mode was the star — bullet points, specific names, actionable advice.
